question_title: How many cumulative confirmed Ebola cases will the DRC have according to ECDC's weekly threats report published around July 10, 2026?
summary: DRC cumulative confirmed Ebola (Bundibugyo) cases in the ECDC CDTR whose week ends on/before 10 July 2026 — this is the Week 28 report (3–10 July, published ~Fri 10 July). Base: 1,274 confirmed as of 27 June (ECDC site). Growth has been remarkably linear at ~38.75/day over 24 days (344 on 3 Jun → 1,274 on 27 Jun). The CDTR cites data with ~2-day lag (Week 26 ended 26 Jun, cited 1,155 = 24 Jun data), so Week 28 likely cites ~8 July data, ~11 days forward: 1,274 + 40×11 ≈ 1,714. Monte Carlo with rate-scenario mixture (steady 40, accelerating 50, slowing 28, surge 65), data-cutoff/report-timing uncertainty, and an 8% backlog-dump bolus gives median ~1,723, P10 1,508, P90 1,960. Right tail extended for epidemic acceleration/testing scale-up; left tail covers Week 27 fallback and PHEIC-driven slowing. P(>2600) negligible (would require sustained ~120/day, 3× current). Distribution sits inside the 1,150–2,600 open bounds.

factors.description: Growth remarkably linear at ~39/day for 24 days (344 on 3 Jun to 1,274 on 27 Jun), enabling reliable short-horizon projection
Relevant report is Week 28 (ends Fri 10 July), cited data with ~2-day lag => ~8 July cutoff, ~11 days from 27 Jun base
Outbreak in 'upward phase' expanding to new health zones; acceleration / backlog dumps / testing scale-up push right tail higher
PHEIC response (vaccination, treatment) could slow growth; or Week 28 report delayed forcing Week 27 (~1-3 July) data => lower value

reasoning:
I've submitted my forecast.

**Summary:** The question asks for DRC cumulative confirmed Ebola cases in the ECDC CDTR Week 28 report (3–10 July, published ~Fri 10 July).

**Key reasoning:**
- **Base:** 1,274 confirmed as of 27 June (latest ECDC data)
- **Trajectory:** Remarkably linear at ~39/day over 24 days — unusually predictable
- **Data lag:** ECDC CDTR cites data ~2 days behind week-end (Week 26 ended 26 Jun citing 24 Jun data), so Week 28 likely reflects ~8 July figures (~11 days forward)
- **Central estimate:** ~1,723 (1,274 + ~40/day × 11 days)

**Distribution (Monte Carlo):** median **1,723**, P10 **1,508**, P90 **1,960**. Right tail extended for epidemic acceleration/backlog dumps; left tail covers a Week 27 fallback or PHEIC-driven slowdown. Reaching the creator's upper bound of 2,600 would require sustained ~120/day (3× current rate), so P(>2,600) is negligible.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the cumulative number of laboratory-confirmed Ebola disease cases in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC only, excluding Uganda or any other country) as stated in the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) whose reporting week ends closest to, but on or before, 10 July 2026, accessible from the ECDC weekly threats reports index at https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/publications-and-data/monitoring/weekly-threats-reports. The relevant figure is the most recent cumulative confirmed-case total for the DRC in that report's Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) section. For example, a report stating "1,500 confirmed cases" in the DRC resolves this question as 1500.
fine_print: "Confirmed cases" means laboratory-confirmed cases only; suspected and probable cases are excluded. Only cases attributed to the DRC are counted; cases in Uganda, France, or any other country are excluded. If the CDTR reports a cumulative DRC total along with separate provincial breakdowns, the stated cumulative national total is used. If the relevant CDTR edition reports the DRC figure as a date-stamped value (e.g., "as of 8 July 2026"), that value is used regardless of the exact reporting date. If no ECDC CDTR with a reporting week ending on or before 10 July 2026 and published by 12 July 2026 is available, or it omits a DRC cumulative confirmed-case figure, the question resolves based on the most recent cumulative confirmed-case total for the DRC reported by WHO Disease Outbreak News or other [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) as of 10 July 2026.

factors.description: Growth remarkably linear at ~39/day for 24 days (344 on 3 Jun to 1,274 on 27 Jun), enabling reliable short-horizon projection
Relevant report is Week 28 (ends Fri 10 July), cited data ~2-day lag => ~8 July cutoff, ~11 days from 27 Jun base
Outbreak in 'upward phase' with expansion to new health zones; possible acceleration/backlog dumps/testing scale-up push right tail higher
PHEIC response (vaccination, treatment) could slow growth; or Week 28 report delayed forcing Week 27 (~1 July) data => lower value

input.anchor: Linear extrapolation: 1,274 (27 Jun) + ~40/day x ~11 days to ~8 Jul data cutoff = ~1,714
input.assessment: Numeric forecast for DRC cumulative confirmed Ebola cases in ECDC CDTR Week 28 (ends 10 July 2026). Base: 1,274 confirmed as of 27 June (ECDC site). Growth extremely stable at ~39-40/day over 24 days. Relevant report cites data with ~2-day lag (Week 26 ended 26 Jun, cited 1,155 = 24 Jun data), so Week 28 likely cites ~8 July data = 11 days forward => ~1,274 + 40x11 = ~1,714. Monte Carlo with rate-scenario mixture (steady 40, accelerating 50, slowing 28, surge 65) and data-cutoff/report uncertainty gives median ~1,723, P10 1,508, P90 1,960. Right tail extended for epidemic acceleration and backlog-dump risk; left tail covers Week 27 fallback and control measures. P(>2600) negligible (would need sustained 120/day, 3x current). Open bounds; distribution fully inside 1,150-2,600.
input.key_uncertainties: Exact data cutoff date the Week 28 CDTR will cite (July 6-10); whether linear growth holds or accelerates/decelerates; small chance Week 28 not published by 12 July forcing Week 27 fallback.
input.tool_audit: research() returned excellent up-to-date data (1,274 as of 27 Jun, CDTR publishing schedule Fridays, Week 26 figures). execute_code ran Monte Carlo cleanly. No failures.
input.process_reflection: Smooth workflow. The research sub-agent nailed exactly what I needed (latest count + CDTR publishing cadence + lag pattern). The main modeling challenge was the data-cutoff/lag question, which research resolved. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
All four factors are grounded in the research trace, and the numeric estimate is well-supported.

VERIFICATION:
- Factor 1 (linear ~39/day): Confirmed by execute_code output — full-span avg 38.75/day over 24 days (344→1,274), segment rates 37-41/day. Grounded.
- Factor 2 (Week 28 report, ~2-day lag → ~8 July cutoff): Research confirms CDTR weekly on Fridays; Week 26 (ends 26 June) cited 1,155 ≈ 24 June data, supporting the ~2-day lag inference. Reasonable.
- Factor 3 (upward phase / new health zones): Research states "no clear plateau," "sustained, slightly accelerating expansion," with multi-province spread. Grounded.
- Factor 4 (PHEIC response / Week 27 fallback): PHEIC confirmed (17 May 2026); fallback logic sound; negative logit correctly matches the downward direction described.

RESOLUTION ALIGNMENT: Factors engage the specific criteria — ECDC CDTR as source, DRC-only (correctly excludes Uganda's 20 cases), confirmed-only, the "week ending on or before 10 July" logic, and the date-stamped-value fine print (modeled via data-cutoff uncertainty). Good alignment.

PRE-PUBLICATION EVENT: The base (1,274 as of 27 June) predates published_at (29 June) but is correctly used only as a launch point for forward projection to a future (~10 July) report, not treated as already resolving the question. No violation.

MATH/DISTRIBUTION: Anchor 1,274 + 40×11 ≈ 1,714 matches the stated anchor; Monte Carlo median 1,723 (P10 1,508, P90 1,960) matches the tentative estimate exactly. My independent estimate aligns — base + ~11 days × ~40/day ≈ 1,714 with appropriate two-sided uncertainty. No meaningful divergence.

WORLDVIEW CONSISTENCY: Fresh research entry (dfee26f1) is fully consistent with the factors; no contradictions. The one Ebola archive entry (46f85a72) is superseded/stale and provides only historical base rates, no contradiction. No prior CDTR sub-forecasts exist for binary-vs-numeric checks.

TOOL AUDIT: Agent claims "No failures." Ground truth shows 0 errors / 17 calls. The trace's initial `research` tool-name error was an immediate self-corrected wrong-name retry, not counted in metrics; the audit claim is consistent.

MINOR (non-flaggable): The right tail may be marginally thin for an outbreak in acceleration phase with backlog-dump risk, but that is a magnitude judgment, not an error. Confidence 0.70 is well-calibrated given genuine cutoff-date/report-timing uncertainty. The counterargument is substantive (three concrete alternative scenarios with evidentiary basis), not a token gesture.

No fabricated claims, no double-counting, no wrong-direction factors, no resolution misalignment. Approve.
